per_plan.coverage_boundary: Startup alignment covers ordinary runtime and unit tests for the startup gate. It does not rerun UI smoke or long parent graphs.

obligations.description: Startup asks the three questions, waits for answers, and blocks banner/controller work until the answer boundary is satisfied.
Startup creates prompt-isolated current-run state and requires reviewer facts plus PM approval before work beyond startup.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Packet/card/ACK alignment covers mechanical handoff, identity, hash, receipt, and ACK/return hazards. It does not treat ACK as semantic role approval.

obligations.description: Packet envelopes, bodies, result files, hashes, and recipient reads stay physically separated from Controller relay text.
Card and bundle ACKs require correct role, token, receipt, hash, and per-card completion before advancing.
Router preconsumes valid ACK/return events and blocks incomplete or invalid pre-ACK reports without advancing stale waits.
Packet control-plane model keeps its facade import path while state, transition, and invariant owners remain executable.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Route-mutation alignment covers activation preconditions, sibling replacement, stale evidence, and route-sign projection for ordinary tests.

obligations.description: Route mutation declares topology, affected nodes, process/product rechecks, and old packet supersession before activation.
Sibling branch replacement marks old sibling evidence stale and projects replacement/replay scope in the route display.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Terminal, closure, and resume alignment covers runtime tests for ledger closure and re-entry. It is not a production replay adapter.

obligations.description: Terminal completion requires a clean PM-built final ledger, delivered-product backward replay, and PM completion approval.
Closure blocks dirty defect, PM-suggestion, role-memory, quarantine, or source-of-truth state after terminal replay.
Resume re-entry loads current-run state, frontier, packet ledger, daemon/owner state, role memory, and recovery evidence before PM work.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Role/output alignment covers mechanical role-output and packet-output contracts. It does not judge task semantic quality.

obligations.description: Role outputs are registry-bound, role-authored, authority-checked, and submitted only through current Router waits.
Output contracts are repeated in packet envelope/body/ledger/result and rejected for wrong recipients.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Router-loop/daemon alignment covers foreground unit/runtime tests for queue, lock, and packet-loop behavior. It does not run long daemon soak tests.

obligations.description: The current-node loop dispatches packets only after direct-dispatch preflight and routes results through PM/reviewer gates.
The persistent Router daemon owns one run lock, records status, waits on fresh locks, and does not reactivate released locks.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Test-tiering alignment covers test-plan mechanics and slow-test parent/child contracts, including progress-only background hazards.

obligations.description: Fast/router tiers are scoped, foreground-safe, and exclude release-only, coverage sweep, and legacy full regressions.
Background validation needs final out/err/combined/exit/meta artifacts; progress lines alone are not pass evidence.
Slow-test parents declare child owners and I/O contracts and do not replay child internals as parent proof.

per_plan.coverage_boundary: Meta/Capability alignment covers parent-runner proof boundaries and control-gate unit evidence only. Parent results remain abstract model confidence, not ordinary production conformance.

obligations.description: Meta parent routine evidence uses thin-parent proof by default and layered full proof only when explicitly requested.
Capability parent evidence rejects stale proof reuse and keeps fast smoke on the non-legacy path.
Meta/Capability parent model evidence stays bounded as abstract model-hierarchy confidence, not ordinary production conformance.

source_audit_boundary: Source-contract alignment is a conservative AST-supported subset of critical externally visible Python surfaces. It proves that selected tests directly call the declared code contract symbols and assert their external boundary. It does not replace the broader declaration alignment, runtime conformance replay, or long FlowGuard regressions.

obligations.description: Source-audited startup boundary for run-until-wait, action application, and next-action answer handling.
Source-audited startup activation failure boundary through recorded external events.
Source-audited Controller handoff boundary that keeps packet bodies out of Controller relay text.
Source-audited card open/ACK/validation contract for externally visible card acknowledgement mechanics.
Source-audited ACK preconsumption boundary through Router events and card ACK helpers.
Source-audited route mutation precondition boundary through Router events and packet issuance.
Source-audited sibling replacement boundary through Router events, packet issuance, and route-sign output.
Source-audited terminal replay/final-ledger boundary through Router event intake.
Source-audited resume re-entry boundary through Router event intake, next action, and action application.
Source-audited role-output session preparation contract.
Source-audited packet output contract across packet creation, Controller relay, and result writes.
Source-audited current-node packet/result review loop boundary through Router event intake.
Source-audited officer request/result lifecycle records keep PM authority, sealed-body boundaries, and validation results explicit.
Source-audited continuation quarantine, final user report, and route display refresh records separate current-run authority from display/report artifacts.
Source-audited daemon lock/status/tick contracts prevent duplicate writers and distinguish live, stale, stopped, and errored daemon states.
Source-audited startup-daemon liveness helpers classify lock freshness and heartbeat monitor requirements.
Source-audited runtime owner modules expose stable owner-boundary helpers with concrete output and failure contracts.
Source-audited router owner helper modules expose stable boundaries behind the compatibility facade.
Source-audited packet runtime owner helpers preserve schema, path, hash, contract, and audit boundaries.
Source-audited packet control-plane runner returns a FlowGuard report-derived exit status.
Source-audited card runtime owner modules preserve bundle, envelope, ledger, path, and hash contracts behind the compatibility facade.
Source-audited packet runtime owner modules preserve startup packet, holder/progress, ledger, CLI parsing, and session metadata contracts.
Source-audited packet control-plane model state helpers map case ids to packet boundary conditions.
Source-audited role-output runtime owner modules preserve contract skeleton, CLI, schema boundary, and envelope recovery contracts.
Source-audited test-tier command selection boundary.
Source-audited Meta runner entrypoint for thin-parent default evidence.
Source-audited smoke fast-path entrypoint for Capability proof boundary evidence.

source_contract_plan.source_audit_boundary: Source-contract alignment is a conservative AST-supported subset of critical externally visible Python surfaces. It proves that selected tests directly call the declared code contract symbols and assert their external boundary. It does not replace the broader declaration alignment, runtime conformance replay, or long FlowGuard regressions.

test evidence source_bad.no_assert calls no assertion for the external contract

source for source_bad.extra_effect has side-effect-looking calls not declared by the code contract
